title: Coffee Field Model: Sequential Action Planning
text: functional field model situational forces propagate environment produce action outcomes. moment field specifies forces afforded — causal pathways open given current state situation. Forces spread step--step: disturbance time t transmitted field produce new state time t + 1, opens (closes) pathways available t + 2. field F supports two complementary workflows. Induction runs data → F: fit field’s coefficients observed behaviour pathXMY() friends. Deduction runs F → behaviour: treat coefficients working theory simulate forward see plan produces. vignette second direction. notation uses throughout — F / F_t field objects fZ_X.Y parameter schema — see vignette("notation", package = "funfield"). worked example making cup drip coffee: four-step action sequence becomes causally connected field. key idea causal gating — later step effect earlier step changed situation. plan supplies intention act stage, situation controls whether acting anything.

title: Coffee Field Model: Sequential Action Planning
text: functional field iterates Ft=F(st),st+1=st⋅Ft, F_t = F(s_t), \qquad s_{t+1} = s_t \cdot F_t, s_t situational state (named vector node values) F_t field evaluated state. Two helpers run loop: evalF() takes one step: given field (lavaan model string), parameter values, current s_t, returns s_{t+1}. runF() drives iteration fixed number steps returns full trajectory. (can also inject externally scheduled actions, let plan drive — see Step 2.) build field two halves lavaan lets us stitch together simple concatenation: situation — affordance structure world, exists whether anyone acts — action plan — set conditional intentions agent layers top.

ESJT data L rated likelihood taking action, plays role economist give subjective expected utility: field assumes person takes action appraise positively — one see, level, carrying highest utility. Reading L settled field therefore reading action agent predicted choose. Node names lavaan-safe (letters, digits, _ ); . inside parameter label source/target separator. need type labels hand: labelF() derives fZ_X.Y label term captures coefficient fix inline matching params vector. labelling order-dependent reads straight right-hand side — Y ~ Z:X becomes fZ_X.Y — interaction written condition first, action last: gating state, action affords. fix force 1 everywhere, wave propagates without attenuation (smaller values damp travels chain). Passing actions = lets labelF() check action slipped condition slot: labelF() returns relabelled model string params. interaction reads condition → action: s2:TurnOn force action TurnOn HCoPot, afforded state s2, labelled fs2_TurnOn.HCoPot. lone baseline term L ~ HCo condition becomes f1_HCo.L — always active HCo present. title: Coffee Field Model: Sequential Action Planning
text: plotField() draws field single state node-link diagram. Following field-diagram convention, object nodes (situational states) diamonds, action nodes right-facing triangles, terminal likelihood node left-facing triangle. node shaded current value — white 0, blue 1 — arrow drawn every force currently afforded: resolved field coefficient params[label] * s[gate] collapses afforded force 1 · X:Z 0 (gate shut, edge) 1 (gate open, edge present) level gating state Z known. Passing plan = plan colours forces action plan contributes gold, distinct situation’s forces grey — standing convention “forces added conditional action plan.” Laid staircase, two interleave: gold edges horizontal steps (state handing action triggers) grey edges diagonal steps (action producing outcome). node spacing chosen two kinds arrow stay close length staircase climbs steeply enough fill roughly square frame rather long thin strip: gold state → action steps span two horizontal units, grey action → outcome steps span one horizontal two vertical units: Stepping plotField() across rows trajectory stitching frames plotsAsWidget() gives interactive back/forward animation. Use ← Back / Forward → watch forces appear outcome attained nodes turn blue state propagates staircase: t = 0 entire plan already laid gold — agent holds four conditional intentions start — make → rig set (afforded agent begins ready, s1 = 1) baseline coffee → L live among grey situation forces. wave climbs, newly-attained object opens next situation force, fresh grey arrow appears one stage , nodes turn blue action gate finally fires.

title: Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the coworker assertiveness ESJT
text: Deviate Level-1 columns within (person, situation) devPSI(), merge condition variable code manager dummy (1 = coworker manager, 0 = coworker subordinate).
code: mediators <- c("Appropriate","MajorArg","GoodRelationP",                "Punished","CompleteJob","P_Punished","CompanySuccess")  ## devPSI() deviates each action's outcome ratings within (person, situation): ## each rating is centered on the person's mean across the actions they rated ## in that same situation, isolating the within-situation action contrast. dat <- merge(devPSI(coworkerESJT$PSI), coworkerESJT$cond, by = c("p","s")) dat$manager <- as.numeric(dat$condition == "manager")

code: sit_sub <- coworkerESJT$sit[, c("p","s","WasUnethical")] dat3 <- merge(devPSI(coworkerESJT$PSI), sit_sub, by = c("p","s")) dat3 <- dat3[!is.na(dat3$WasUnethical), ] cat("Rows:", nrow(dat3), " | Persons:", length(unique(dat3$p)), "\n") #> Rows: 4760  | Persons: 206 mod_ue <- pathXMY(dat3, X = "ExpDisapproval", Y = "Likelihood",                   M = mediators, Z = "WasUnethical", Z.within = TRUE)

what: Does perceived unethicality moderate the expectation arm (ExpDisapproval → M)?
title: Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the coworker assertiveness ESJT
text: Moderation expectation paths perceived unethicality (BZ_MX) Six seven mediators significantly moderated (P_Punished exception), effects substantially larger coworker rank. dominant pattern Appropriate (BZ_MX = 1.45, z = 23.4): situations perceived unethical respondent’s personal average, expressing disapproval expected dramatically role-appropriate. complementary picture holds Punished (BZ_MX = −0.71): coworker’s behavior perceived unethical, respondent expects less personal punishment pushing back — moral justification removes sense personal risk. CompleteJob (0.60) CompanySuccess (0.80) also show large positive moderation: unethical situations, expressing disapproval expected serve task-completion organizational interests far better. profile near-complete reversal manager-rank effect: higher-power coworker suppresses perceived benefits asserting, perceived unethicality amplifies .

code: ## Local helpers for the three moderation schematics below. Wrap a ## fragment in limegreen to flag it as a bZ coefficient, and in light ## gray to fade it into "not part of this term". ## Note: starred-total betas use * (HTML numeric entity for `*`) ## rather than a literal asterisk -- gridtext treats paired `*` ## characters as Markdown italic, which would break color spans that ## happen to bracket them. green <- "#32CD32"; gray <- "#E5E5E5" gz <- function(s) paste0("", s, "") gr <- function(s) paste0("", s, "") b1_MX <- "β1MX" b1_YM <- "β1YM" b1_YX <- "β1YX" bZ_MX <- "βZMX" bZ_YM <- "βZYM" bZ_YX <- "βZYX" b1s_YX <- "β*1YX" # starred b1 (total) bZs_YX <- "β*ZYX" # starred bZ (total) plotPathSchema( XY_label = paste0(b1s_YX, " ", gz(paste0("+ ", bZs_YX))), X_shape = "rtTri", Y_shape = "lfTri" ) plotPathSchema( M_label = "M", XM_label = paste0(b1_MX, " ", gz(paste0("+ ", bZ_MX))), MY_label = paste0(b1_YM, " ", gz(paste0("+ ", bZ_YM))), XY_label = paste0(b1_YX, " ", gz(paste0("+ ", bZ_YX))), X_shape = "rtTri", Y_shape = "lfTri" ) ## Helper: stitch a single XY-label string for an arm where one of ## (b1, bZ) is active and the other is faded. arm_lab <- function(b1, bZ, active = c("b1", "bZ")) { active <- match.arg(active) if (active == "b1") paste0(b1, " ", gr(paste0("+ ", bZ))) else paste0(gr(b1), " ", gz(paste0("+ ", bZ))) } ## And a fully faded label (whole "b1 + bZ" string wrapped in gray). arm_off <- function(b1, bZ) gr(paste0(b1, " + ", bZ)) ## Panel 1: BZ_MX * B1_YM (expectation-route moderation). p1 <- plotPathSchema( M_label = "M", XM_label = arm_lab(b1_MX, bZ_MX, "bZ"), # bZ active -> green line MY_label = arm_lab(b1_YM, bZ_YM, "b1"), # b1 active -> black line XY_label = arm_off(b1_YX, bZ_YX), # not in this term X_shape = "rtTri", Y_shape = "lfTri", XM_color = green, MY_color = "black", XY_color = gray, title = "Term 1: βZMX · β1YM" ) + ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown(hjust = 0.5)) ## Panel 2: B1_MX * BZ_YM (valuation-route moderation). p2 <- plotPathSchema( M_label = "M", XM_label = arm_lab(b1_MX, bZ_MX, "b1"), MY_label = arm_lab(b1_YM, bZ_YM, "bZ"), XY_label = arm_off(b1_YX, bZ_YX), X_shape = "rtTri", Y_shape = "lfTri", XM_color = "black", MY_color = green, XY_color = gray, title = "Term 2: β1MX · βZYM" ) + ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown(hjust = 0.5)) ## Panel 3: BZ_YX (residual direct moderation). p3 <- plotPathSchema( M_label = "M", XM_label = arm_off(b1_MX, bZ_MX), MY_label = arm_off(b1_YM, bZ_YM), XY_label = arm_lab(b1_YX, bZ_YX, "bZ"), X_shape = "rtTri", Y_shape = "lfTri", XM_color = gray, MY_color = gray, XY_color = green, title = "Term 3: βZYX" ) + ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown(hjust = 0.5)) patchwork::wrap_plots(p1, p2, p3, ncol = 3) ## Same three frames as Figure 3, but stitched into a Back/Forward ## widget so they swap at pixel-identical layout instead of sitting ## side by side. p1/p2/p3 are reused from the chunk above. plotsAsWidget( list(p1, p2, p3), panel_titles = c( "Term 1: B_Z_MX * B_1_YM (expectation route)", "Term 2: B_1_MX * B_Z_YM (valuation route)", "Term 3: B_Z_YX (residual direct)" ), width = 7, height = 3.5, format = "svg" )

code: joint_yx <- subset(mod_p$tidy_joint, param %in% c("B1_YX_joint","BZ_YX_joint")) joint_yx$mediator <- NULL kable0(joint_yx, digits = 3,   caption = "Residual direct path X -> Y from the joint fit of all 8 mediators (Z = SRFastDriver)") loop <- subset(mod_p$tidy_loop,                param == "BZ_YM" & !is.na(mediator))[, c("mediator","est","se")] join <- subset(mod_p$tidy_joint,                param == "BZ_YM_joint")[, c("mediator","est","se")] names(loop)[2:3] <- c("loop_est","loop_se") names(join)[2:3] <- c("joint_est","joint_se") cmp <- merge(loop, join, by = "mediator") cmp$se_inflation <- cmp$joint_se / cmp$loop_se kable0(cmp, digits = 3,   caption = "BZ_YM (valuation moderation) per mediator: loop vs joint, with SE inflation factor")

code: screen <- screenModerators(psi    = dat,                            X      = "Speed",                            Y      = "Likelihood",                            M      = mediators,                            traits = speedingESJT$traits) #> screenModerators: skipping non-numeric trait columns: employment, education, income, ethnicity, gender  ## Attach the codebook label so the table is readable. labels <- speedingESJT$codebook$trait_items[, c("var","label")] screen <- merge(screen, labels,                 by.x = "trait", by.y = "var", all.x = TRUE, sort = FALSE)  screen_yx <- subset(screen, target == "YX") screen_yx <- screen_yx[order(-abs(screen_yx$beta)), ] kable0(head(screen_yx, 15)[, c("trait","r","beta","p","label")],              digits = 3, row.names = FALSE,              caption = paste0("Top 15 traits ranked by the within-person ",                               "Speed effect on Likelihood: a fast screen ",                               "for total-moderation candidates.")) ## Balanced 2-level within-person design: pull raw PSI (since `dat` ## has Speed within-person-deviated for the pathXMY pipeline) and ## drop the middle Speed level so each person contributes the ## endpoint difference L|Speed=1 - L|Speed=0. d2 <- subset(speedingESJT$PSI, Speed != 0.5) d2 <- merge(d2,             transform(speedingESJT$traits[, c("p","SR_30")],                       SRz = as.numeric(scale(SR_30)))[, c("p","SRz")],             by = "p") d2 <- d2[order(d2$p, d2$Speed), ]  ## (A) per-person Δ regression -- the "screen" form, 1 row per person dL  <- tapply(d2$Likelihood, d2$p, function(L) L[2] - L[1]) SRz <- tapply(d2$SRz,        d2$p, `[`, 1) coef(summary(lm(dL ~ SRz)))["SRz", ] #>     Estimate   Std. Error      t value     Pr(>|t|)  #> 2.536665e-01 2.534038e-02 1.001037e+01 1.245500e-20  ## (A') what screenModerators() actually calls -- Pearson cor.test on ## the same Δ vector. The `estimate` it returns is `r` (the ## standardized form of b₂; SD(dL) cancels into SD-units) but the ## *t*-statistic and *p*-value are the same hypothesis test. ct <- cor.test(dL, SRz) c(r = unname(ct$estimate), t = unname(ct$statistic), p = ct$p.value) #>            r            t            p  #> 4.936504e-01 1.001037e+01 1.245500e-20  ## (B) same numbers from person fixed effects on the 2N-row form coef(summary(lm(Likelihood ~ Speed * SRz + factor(p),                 data = d2)))["Speed:SRz", ] #>     Estimate   Std. Error      t value     Pr(>|t|)  #> 2.536665e-01 2.534038e-02 1.001037e+01 1.245500e-20 screen_ym <- subset(screen, target == "YM") screen_ym <- screen_ym[order(-abs(screen_ym$beta)), ] kable0(head(screen_ym, 10)[, c("trait","mediator","beta","p","label")],              digits = 3, row.names = FALSE,              caption = paste0("Top 10 valuation-arm (YM) moderation ",                               "candidates: trait moderation of the ",                               "M -> Likelihood path."))

text: raw arms tell story decomposition, one step upstream. expectation side (BZ_MX), time pressure significantly amplifies expected -time gain speeding (OnTime, p < .001 — largest effect) expected meeting-quality gain (IntQuality, p < .001), appropriateness borderline third (Appropriate, p ≈ .05). expectations — including crash ticket expectations — moved. People time pressure don’t expect different consequences speeding; expect larger payoff getting time. valuation side (BZ_YM), nothing reaches p < .05: time pressure detectably change heavily expected outcome weighted. two nearest misses cost outcomes — Ticket (b = -.14, p = .06) MoneyCost (b = -.13, p = .10) — hinting respondents may weight financial costs speeding slightly heavily rushed, neither effect significant. echoes fast-driver case: expectation-arm moderation common case data; valuation-arm moderation comparatively rare.

Condition balance: 3 additional participants removed     rated least one manager one subordinate     scenario, 12 scenarios represented. Final analytic sample: N = 249 participants. Typical workflow. Deviate Level-1 columns within person situation devPSI() fitting path models — centers action's rating person's mean across actions rated situation, isolating within-situation action contrast: Using condition moderator. devPSI() deviates Level-1 columns within (p, s); pathXMY call uses Z.within = TRUE situation-level moderator within-person deviated instead (constant within (p, s), must deviated person level):
code: data(coworkerESJT) dev <- devPSI(coworkerESJT$PSI) dat <- merge(devPSI(coworkerESJT$PSI), coworkerESJT$cond, by = c("p","s")) dat$manager <- as.numeric(dat$condition == "manager") res <- pathXMY(dat, X = "ExpDisapproval", Y = "Likelihood",                M = "Appropriate", Z = "manager", Z.within = TRUE)

Default `NULL` (check). default Value assigned `params` terms neither carry fixed coefficient existing label--value. Default `NA`.

title: Auto-generate `fZ_X.Y` parameter labels for a field model — labelF
text: list two elements: `model` relabelled model string: every regression term       rewritten `label * rhs`. `params` named numeric vector, one entry per generated /       retained label, holding fixed value input       supplied one `default` otherwise. Suitable passing straight [runF()] / [evalF()] (  `$model`, `$params`), stitching two halves   `paste($model, b$model)` `c($params, b$params)`.

title: Auto-generate `fZ_X.Y` parameter labels for a field model — labelF
text: Labelling **order-dependent**, order read straight right-hand side: `Y ~ Z:X` becomes `fZ_X.Y`. interaction written **condition first, action last** — leading variable(s) gating **condition** `Z`, final variable **action** / source `X`, `Y` **target**. generic form `Y ~ Condition:Action`, generating `fCondition_Action.Y`. term single right-hand variable (`Y ~ X`) carries condition becomes baseline `f1_X.Y`; multiple leading conditions concatenated (matching compound `fab_X.Y` convention). Variable names used verbatim (case change), labels round-trip cleanly. order load-bearing, conditions must precede actions. `actions` supplied, `labelF()` warns one action variables turns condition (non-final) position — sign term written `Y ~ Action:Condition` mistake. fixed coefficient (`1 * Z:X`, `c * Z:X`) ** obstacle: label generated exactly free term, fixed value captured parameter's entry `params`. example, row `s2 ~ 1 * s1:make` becomes `s2 ~ fs1_make.s2 * s1:make` `params["fs1_make.s2"] = 1`. Terms left free unfixed get `params` value `default` (`NA` default), filled model run. Terms already carry label keep .

what: Path Analysis for X -> M -> Y Structures with Optional Moderation — pathXMY
title: Path Analysis for X -> M -> Y Structures with Optional Moderation — pathXMY
text: Fits path model $$M = \beta^1_{MX} X + \beta^Z_{MX}(X \times Z) + \beta^Z_M Z + e_M$$ $$Y = \beta^1_{YX} X + \beta^Z_{YX}(X \times Z) + \beta^1_{YM} M + \beta^Z_{YM}(M \times Z) + \beta^Z_Y Z + e_Y$$ via lavaan cluster-robust standard errors (clustering cluster, typically person ID "p" ESJT data). Omitting M collapses direct \(X \Y\) model (optionally moderated). Omitting Z collapses unmoderated version. Supplying multiple variable names M loops model mediator one time returns single stacked tidy table.

title: Path Analysis for X -> M -> Y Structures with Optional Moderation — pathXMY
code: pathXMY(   data,   X,   Y,   M = NULL,   Z = NULL,   Z.within = FALSE,   cluster = "p",   situation = "s",   controls = NULL,   deviate = c("auto", "none"),   se = c("cluster", "boot"),   nboot = 500,   conf.level = 0.95,   check.deviation = TRUE,   suppress.warnings = TRUE,   joint = TRUE )

title: Path Analysis for X -> M -> Y Structures with Optional Moderation — pathXMY
text: data data frame PSI long format (p, s, first columns). default Level-1 columns X, M, Y within-(person, situation) deviated automatically (see deviate); Z handled separately. X Name initiating action / predictor variable (length-1 character). Y Name outcome variable (length-1 character; typically "L" likelihood). M Optional. Name(s) candidate mediator variables (character vector). NULL, direct \(X \Y\) model fit (mediator). length > 1, X-M-Y model fit per mediator. Z Optional. Name moderator variable (length-1 character). NULL, unmoderated model fit. default (Z.within = FALSE), Z treated -person trait z-standardized globally. Set Z.within = TRUE situation-level moderator varies within persons (e.g., experimentally varied condition); case Z within-cluster deviated instead. Z.within Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, Z treated within-cluster (situation-level) variable within-person deviated fitting, exactly like Level-1 predictors. Use moderator varies across situations within person (e.g., randomly assigned scenario condition). FALSE (default), Z z-standardized globally (-person trait interpretation). cluster Name clustering variable (default "p"). Set NULL fit without cluster-robust SEs (recommended ESJT data). situation Name situation/scenario column (default "s"), paired cluster define (person, situation) cells used automatic deviation. controls Optional character vector control variables added equations. deviate One "auto" (default) "none". "auto", X, M, Y within-(person, situation) deviated fitting — value centered mean actions rated (cluster, situation) cell — provided situation column present every cell holds least two rows. cells hold single action data left untouched (deviate first, e.g. devPSI). "none", deviation applied pre-deviated data assumed. se One "cluster" (default; sandwich SEs lavaan) "boot" (cluster bootstrap nboot replicates). nboot Number bootstrap replicates se = "boot". conf.level Confidence level CI columns (default 0.95). check.deviation Logical. TRUE (default), warn X, M, Y still look within-person deviated deviate step — relevant mainly deviate = "none" data pre-deviated. suppress.warnings Logical. TRUE (default), suppress cosmetic non-PD vcov warning lavaan emits machine precision. joint Logical (default TRUE). length(M) > 1, additionally fit single joint multi-mediator model mediators appear simultaneously Y equation. joint fit contributes residual \(X \Y\) path (controlling full mediator set), parallel per-mediator coefficients suffix _joint. See Details.

title: Path Analysis for X -> M -> Y Structures with Optional Moderation — pathXMY
text: list three elements: tidy_loop tidy data frame one row per (mediator x       parameter), assembled separate single-mediator       regressions — one X-M-Y fit per mediator, fit independently.       Y equation row's source model contains exactly one       mediator. coefficients simultaneous       regression read partial effects net       mediators. Columns: mediator, param,       est, se, z, pvalue, ci.lower,       ci.upper. Parameter labels follow XMY convention:       B1_MX, BZ_MX, B1_YX, BZ_YX,       B1_YM, BZ_YM, plus indirect effects       B1_MX * B1_YM (unmoderated ),       BZ_MX * B1_YM (\(= \beta^Z_{MX} \cdot \beta^1_{YM}\)),       B1_MX * BZ_YM (\(= \beta^1_{MX} \cdot \beta^Z_{YM}\)).       M = NULL, slot holds rows single       Y--X direct regression (mediator = NA). tidy_joint tidy data frame single       simultaneous multi-mediator regression mediators       appear together Y equation. NULL       joint = FALSE length(M) <= 1. Per-mediator rows       (B1_MX_joint, BZ_MX_joint, B1_YM_joint,       BZ_YM_joint) indexed mediator; global rows       (B1_YX_joint, BZ_YX_joint) carry mediator = NA.       B*_YM_joint coefficients partial slopes net       mediators comparable       single-mediator B*_YM values tidy_loop. fits named list lavaan fit objects (one per mediator,       named mediator variable name; "_direct"       M = NULL; "_joint" joint multi-mediator fit       present).

title: Path Analysis for X -> M -> Y Structures with Optional Moderation — pathXMY
text: Deviation. cluster = "p" lavaan provides cluster-robust sandwich standard errors single-level SEM; multilevel model. Correct within-person path coefficients therefore require Level-1 variables (X, M, Y) deviated within (cluster, situation) cell. default (deviate = "auto") pathXMY() internally; pass deviate = "none" data already deviated. one action rated per (person, situation), within-cell deviation isolates within-situation action contrast field model ; single situation per person reduces ordinary within-person deviation. Z handled separately: -person trait moderator (Z.within = FALSE) z-standardized, situation-level moderator (Z.within = TRUE) within-person deviated. Small cluster counts. cluster-robust z-tests assume number clusters \(G\) large. \(G < 50\), results may anti-conservative; consider se = "boot". Naming convention. Parameters use X-M-Y suffix convention Wood, Harms, & Cho (2023): B1_* main-effect coefficients, BZ_* Z-moderated coefficients; B*_MX parameters regress M X, B*_YM regress Y M, B*_YX (direct) Y X coefficients. Loop vs joint fits — two different regressions, two different tables. multiple mediators, pathXMY() produces two separate tidy tables, deliberately kept apart answer different questions coefficients interchangeable: $tidy_loop comes "loop" pass: one independent         X-M-Y model fit per mediator,         single mediator Y equation. row labeled B1_YM         mediator \(m_k\) slope Y \(m_k\)         controlling X — nothing else. loop pass         inferential workhorse supports stable         expectation-route summary BZ_MX * B1_YM. $tidy_joint comes single simultaneous regression         mediators appear together Y equation.         row labeled B1_YM_joint mediator \(m_k\)         partial slope Y \(m_k\) net every         mediator M. comparable loop         B1_YM values routinely differ magnitude         (sometimes sign) mediators correlated. B1_YX_joint BZ_YX_joint useful joint outputs: index residual direct \(X \Y\) (Z-moderation) controlling entire mediator set, serve diagnostic whether measured mediators absorb total BZ_YX moderation. Per-mediator joint coefficients (especially BZ_YM_joint) typically less stable loop counterparts mediators numerous correlated, M--Z product terms highly collinear (Wood, Adanu, & Harms, 2025). inference single mediator's role, prefer loop coefficient $tidy_loop; treat joint coefficients $tidy_joint system-level diagnostic.

PSI <- speedingESJT$PSI  ## Unmoderated mediation, one mediator res1 <- pathXMY(PSI, X = "Speed", Y = "Likelihood", M = "Crash") res1$tidy_loop  ## Mediation across all eight outcome features mediators <- c("Crash","Injured","Ticket","MoneyCost","OnTime",                "IntQuality","FunDrive","Appropriate") res2 <- pathXMY(PSI, X = "Speed", Y = "Likelihood", M = mediators) ## Loop pass: per-mediator single-mediator regressions res2$tidy_loop ## Joint pass: simultaneous multi-mediator regression res2$tidy_joint } # }

what: Decompose a Z-moderated X-Y effect into expectation-, valuation-, and direct-moderation components — pathXMY_decompose
title: Decompose a Z-moderated X-Y effect into expectation-, valuation-, and direct-moderation components — pathXMY_decompose
text: moderator Z acting link X Y, two models commonly interest: Model [1] direct moderation \(Y = b^1_{YX} X + b^Z_{YX} X     \cdot Z + \dots\). BZ_YX[1] indexes total moderation     effect — much \(Z\) shifts action-outcome link, without     reference mediator. Model [2] full mediated model \(X \M \Y\)     \(Z\) moderating three paths. related algebraic identity (applied one mediator time, linear regression cluster-robust SEs): $$b^Z_{YX}[1] = b^Z_{MX} \cdot b^1_{YM} + b^1_{MX} \cdot b^Z_{YM} + b^Z_{YX}[2]$$ Term 1, BZ_MX * B1_YM: moderation flows     \(Z\) changing expectation \(M\) given \(X\).     pathXMY() highlights default. Term 2, B1_MX * BZ_YM: moderation flows     \(Z\) changing valuation \(M\) driver     \(Y\), leaving expectations untouched. Term 3, BZ_YX[2]: direct \(Z\)-moderation     \(X \Y\) link route mediator. Identifying term carries BZ_YX[1] given mediator answers different psychological question pathXMY() alone. person-trait moderator may leave expectations untouched (BZ_MX near zero) yet substantially shift valuation (BZ_YM large) — invisible BZ_MX table central understanding trait moderates action.

text: Renders functional field `F` evaluated single state `s` ggplot2 node-link diagram. title: Symbolic path-schema diagram (X to Y, optionally through one M) — plotPathSchema
text: Renders clean pedagogical schematic X Y path (optional single mediator M) ggplot2 object. Nodes drawn white shapes (square default; right- left-facing triangle requested via X_shape/Y_shape), edges uniform solid black arrows. Edge labels arbitrary user-supplied strings (typically coefficient symbols) rather fitted numbers, schematic can sit alongside algebraic identity term--term. Two layouts: Total path (M_label = NULL): X left,     Y right, single arrow carrying     XY_label. Mediation triangle (M_label set): X     bottom-left, M apex, Y bottom-right. Edges     X M M Y carry XM_label     MY_label; XY_label also supplied,     direct X Y residual arrow drawn . Edge labels passed geom_richtext, HTML markup supported – e.g. "β<sub>1<sub>MX</sub></sub>" proper Greek nested subscripts.

what: Field-style path diagram for an X-M-Y model — plotPathXMY
title: Field-style path diagram for an X-M-Y model — plotPathXMY
text: Renders X M Y structure ggplot2 object using funfield visualization conventions: Nodes colored expected score X     held 1, continuous diverging gradient (deep red white     deep blue, midpoint 0) mapped [-1, 1] range     via ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient2(). X node     definition 1 (full blue); mediator's score path     coefficient \(b^1_{MX}\); Y's score sum direct     indirect contributions,     \(b^1_{YX} + \sum_j b^1_{YM_j} \cdot b^1_{MX_j}\). Shape defaults true square X     mediator (continuous variables) left-facing triangle Y     (focal action likelihood). X_shape Y_shape     override : use X_shape = "rtTri" X     imagined action (e.g. speeding / overtime EXSJTs),     Y_shape = "square" Y regular continuous variable     rather action likelihood. Mediators always squares. Labels sit outside shapes:     mediator (M), X Y. convention scales     fan view. Edges solid positive paths dashed     negative paths. Linewidth edge color scale path     magnitude (capped scale_max): largest paths     render thick near-black, paths coefficients near     zero fade thin, near-white line recede     background. Arrowheads clipped node perimeter via     shape-aware intersection (L-infinity squares, edge     intersection triangles). Edge labels carry     b1 + bZ(Z) decomposition. Moderator (Z) coloring. Anything purely     bZ coefficient renders limegreen. default decomposed     label, trailing bZ(Z) fragment green (b1     portion edge describe normative path).     Z-overlay view (Z_overlay = TRUE), entire label     edge green, since edge bZ coefficient.     Z-collapsed view (Z_value supplied) renders normally:     label single total slope b1 + bZ * Z_value,     bZ coefficient . Two view modes: Triangle (single mediator) – X bottom-left, M     top, Y bottom-right. Fan (multiple mediators) – X left, mediators     stacked middle, Y right. odd number     mediators stack laid asymmetrically straight     X Y arrow clean gap y = 0 axis. controls fit drives diagram: "loop" (default): per-mediator loop overlay. fan     view overlay intentionally pure – residual direct     X Y arrow drawn, since sensible direct path     picture come different (joint) fit.     single-mediator triangle view, X Y direct path     one mediator's loop residual. "joint": joint multi-mediator simultaneous-fit     picture – mediator's slopes partial slopes net     mediators, X Y arrow single global     B1_YX_joint direct path controlling M. Passing Z_overlay = TRUE renders layout B1 coefficient swapped BZ counterpart – clean view moderator's per-unit effect path weights, isolated normative field. Nodes drawn white view since expected scores meaning per-unit-Z slope. Passing route = "expectation" "valuation" renders per-route view moderation. expectation route shows BZ_MX X--M arm (green) B1_YM M--Y arm (black); valuation route flips arms (B1_MX left, BZ_YM right). visual grammar new coloring scheme makes two route components moderation decomposition read directly: arms mediator carry visible weight one two graphs, green–black black–green chain identifies reason moderator shifts X--Y relationship. Mutually exclusive Z_value Z_overlay; nodes drawn white route views reason Z overlay.

X_label, Y_label, Z_label Display labels X, Y, Z nodes. M_labels Optional character vector labels mediators, parallel mediator (natural order fit). NULL, mediator variable names used. X_shape Shape X node: "square" (default; X regular variable) "rtTri" (right-facing triangle; X imagined action, speeding overtime EXSJTs). text: step `t` `1:steps`, function: 1. Applies actions scheduled step `t` — named state    variable set scheduled value (default `1`) **    propagation. 2. Calls [evalF()] compute `s_(t+1)` post-action `s_t`. 3. Optionally clamps result `[-1, 1]`. `actions` argument list whose names step numbers (character strings, e.g. `"1"`, `"2"`) whose values either: * character vector state-variable names set `1`, * named numeric vector specifying value set variable . Variables never appear LHS `~` `model` (exogenous nodes, typically actions persistent context) carried forward `evalF()` unchanged — action set `1` stays `1` rest run unless clamping later equation overwrites .

YX arm Per person, compute within-person     slope Y X (call \(\Delta Y\)). Across     persons, Pearson correlation \(r(T, \Delta Y)\)     algebraically related BZ_YX[1] -mediator     pathXMY() fit     $$\mathrm{BZ\_YX}[1] = r(T, \Delta Y) \cdot \mathrm{SD}(\Delta Y),$$     pathXMY() z-standardizes -person     moderator internally (\(\mathrm{SD}(T)\) factor     otherwise appear cancels). MX arm shortcut, mediator     place Y: per person compute within-person slope     M_k X (\(\Delta M_k\)) correlate     T. One row per (trait, mediator). YM arm Per-person \(Y\)--\(M\) slopes     sparse typical EXSJT row counts useful (3 obs per     person leaves 0 residual df X partialled ),     YM shortcut computed population level:     single OLS regression within-person-deviated Y     within-person-deviated X M_k     T_z * M_dev interaction. M_dev:T_z     coefficient BZ_YM estimate (matches     pathXMY() ~0.1 percent speeding fit).     reported p-value comes OLS account     within-person clustering, optimistic compared     pathXMY()'s cluster-robust p. Used Wood, Harms, & Cho (2023) canonical example funfield documentation. participant (N = 333 quality screening) rated three actions (drive speed limit, +5 mph, +10+ mph) eight expected outcomes likelihood take action. Six features scenario (vehicle, weather, traffic, time pressure, social context, police visibility) randomly varied across participants, respondent saw exactly one fully crossed instance speeding scenario. differs coworkerESJT, 12 discrete situations rated within participant.

title: Speeding ESJT Dataset — speedingESJT
text: Wood, D., Harms, P. D., & Cho, S. (2023). Speeding study OSF   materials. Raw data captured via Qualtrics; reproduced permission   use canonical example funfield package.

title: Speeding ESJT Dataset — speedingESJT
text: Data cleaning. raw dataset contained 347 participants. Three sequential data-quality screens applied: Straightlining (fancyr::strLine(), cut > 10%     (person, action) rows showing zero variance across 9 rated columns). Low overall variability (fancyr::prMaxSD(), cut     < 0.25 maximum possible SD across 27 rated cells). Response speed (fancyr::spi() combined across     four speeding rating blocks, cut < 1 second per item per Wood,     Harms, Lowman, & DeSimone, 2017). Respondents missing timing data (known intermittent Qualtrics recording failure) retained — missingness screen treated evidence drop. Final analytic sample: N = 333 participants. Rescaling. Likert items stored [0, 1] proportion-- maximum-possible (POMP) scale (fancyr::pomp(x, smin = 1, smax = 5)). Differences two actions [0, 1] outcome therefore fall [-1, 1], natural metric expectancy-value reasoning. Typical workflow. Within-person deviate Level-1 columns fitting path models: Scoring named trait composites. composite recipes $codebook$trait_scales reproduce named scales used Wood, Harms, & Cho (2023):
code: data(speedingESJT) L1 <- c("Speed","MoneyCost","FunDrive","IntQuality","Appropriate",         "OnTime","Crash","Injured","Ticket","Likelihood") dev <- speedingESJT$PSI for (v in L1) dev[[v]] <- ave(dev[[v]], dev$p,                               FUN = function(x) x - mean(x)) tr <- speedingESJT$traits tr$Extra <- rowMeans(cbind(1 - tr$SR_15, tr$SR_16, tr$SR_17), na.rm = TRUE) tr$Open  <- rowMeans(cbind(tr$SR_27, 1 - tr$SR_28, tr$SR_29), na.rm = TRUE) tr$SRFastDriver <- tr$SR_30
